summary.intent: Address a Codex review thread on PR #713 regarding incorrect focus fallback behavior for disabled tags in the TagGroup component, then push the fix and resolve the review cycle.
summary.outcome: Fixed stale explicit focus in TagGroup to not redirect to the first enabled tag (only `Focus(None)` should do that), updated the spec snippet to match, committed as 'Avoid stale disabled tag focus fallback', and pushed. CI passed all checks and the review thread was resolved.

repo: TagGroup focus states must distinguish three cases: `Focus(Some(valid_key))`, `Focus(Some(stale_key))`, and `Focus(None)` — stale explicit focus should be treated as absent focus, not trigger fallback navigation.
Spec snippets in spec/components/data-display/tag-group.md must be kept in sync with implementation and validated via `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` and `cargo xtask spec validate`.
After any push to a PR, a fresh `@codex review` comment must be posted to trigger re-review.
The project uses a spec-conformance test suite: `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance data_display::tag_group::`.

code.finding: Focus transition logic for disabled tags lives here; the fix added a branch separating stale Some(key) from None so only None triggers first-enabled-tag fallback.

workflow: Write a failing regression test before changing the implementation to make the bug explicit and verifiable.
Poll PR reactions and review threads iteratively (60s → 90s → 180s intervals) while waiting for Codex and CI to settle before acting on new threads.
Commit review fixes with a `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer when the fix originates from a Codex review comment.
CI gate ordering: formatting and snapshot checks run first, then Clippy, then longer test/coverage jobs — useful for estimating wait time.

friction: Extended polling wait (60s + 90s + 180s+) before Codex produced a new actionable thread, keeping the session open with no actionable work in the interim.
Codex showed only an `eyes` reaction for a long time with no new threads, making it unclear whether review was in progress or stalled.
open_items: CI coverage jobs were still pending at the time of the final commit — final pass/fail status not confirmed in the transcript.
The earlier spec sync commit (pushed at the start of the session) may warrant a follow-up check once all CI jobs complete.
